title: 10 best herbal tinctures for toothache

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with toothache because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe and calm the affected area. The tincture's ability to reduce inflammation and swelling reduces pressure on the tooth, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause toothaches, promoting healing and restoration of the oral cavity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and mouth, providing relief from toothaches.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and phenolic acids present in pot marigold tincture have antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to combat infections that may be causing the toothache, such as abscesses or gum disease.

Anesthetic and analgesic properties: Pot marigold contains compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can help numb the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with a toothache.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with toothaches by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Sesquiterpenes: They exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help combat the bacteria responsible for tooth decay and gum infections that may lead to toothache pain.
Flavonoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected tooth or gum area, as well as prevent further damage from oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce pain and swelling.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antibacterial properties, which help combat infection and promote healing in the affected area.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh flowers of calendula officinalis in the morning when the dew is still present on the petals.
Clean and dry the harvested calendula flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels.
Steep 1 part of dried calendula flowers in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove the solids.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which ease pain and reduce swelling. The tincture's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to calm the nerves, reducing sensitivity and discomfort caused by tooth decay or gum disease. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the toothache. By soothing the mouth and promoting healing, herbal chamomile tinctures provide effective relief from toothache pain without harsh chemicals or synthetic medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches by reducing inflammation in the mouth and gums.
Antibacterial property: The antimicrobial properties of chamomile, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria, can help combat infections that may be contributing to the toothache.

Soothing and sedative property: Chamomile's calming and sedative effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress related to tooth pain, making it easier for individuals to relax and manage their discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid compound helps with toothache by reducing inflammation and pain through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the affected area and providing temporary relief from toothache pain.

Matricarin: This sesquiterpene lactone compound has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms in the jaw and face associated with toothache.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to relieve toothache pain.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties to help reduce infection and inflammation associated with toothaches.
Seeds: They are used for their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties to help calm toothache pain and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried flowers from matricaria chamomilla at the peak of their potency for maximum effect.
Combine the dried flowers with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar in a ratio of 1:2.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and let it steep for 2-6 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and use 20-30 drops as needed to achieve desired results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic properties, which numb the pain caused by a tooth infection or inflammation. The tannins in wintergreen have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects that help reduce swelling and combat bacterial growth. As a result, wintergreen tincture can provide fast and effective relief from toothache discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for oral health issues. Its antiseptic properties also promote healing and prevent infection from spreading further.
remedy_properties: Methyl salicylate: This compound is responsible for wintergreen's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from toothache pain and reducing swelling.
Gaultherin: This constituent has antibacterial properties, helping to combat the underlying infection or inflammation causing the toothache, while also reducing pain and discomfort.
Tannins: Wintergreen tincture contains high levels of tannins, which help to reduce bleeding, ease gum inflammation, and promote healing in the mouth.
remedy_constituents: Asperuloside: A triterpenoid saponin in Gaultheria procumbens, asperuloside has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with toothache.

Gaultherin: A phenolic compound found in Gaultheria procumbens, gaultherin exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help to alleviate toothache symptoms by reducing inflammation and preventing tissue damage.

Gaultherin's analogs (e.g. gaultherin epoxide): These compounds have been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties similar to gaultherin, which may help to provide pain relief and reduce inflammation associated with toothache.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Gaultheria procumbens contain the highest concentration of essential oils, particularly methyl salicylate, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective for toothache relief.

Leaves: The leaves of Gaultheria procumbens are also used to make tinctures for toothache, as they contain a smaller amount of essential oils, including methyl salicylate, which provides similar relief.
Stems: The stems of Gaultheria procumbens are less commonly used, but still contain essential oils, including methyl salicylate, which may contribute to their use in toothache tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in late spring or early summer when plant is in full leaf.
Chop the collected plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:5.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums and jaw. The citral content in lemongrass also possesses antibacterial qualities, combating bacteria that can cause infections and abscesses, thereby reducing the severity of toothaches. Additionally, lemongrass tincture's analgesic properties provide quick relief from pain, making it a natural and effective remedy for soothing toothache discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass tincture's antibacterial properties help combat bacteria that can cause tooth decay and gum disease, reducing inflammation and pain in the teeth and gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass tincture reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with toothaches, making it a natural remedy for soothing sore gums and teeth.
Antispasmodic properties: Lemongrass tincture's antispasmodic effects help relax muscles and tissues in the mouth, reducing spasms and pain caused by tooth decay or other oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps with toothache by reducing inflammation and pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Geranial: Contributes to toothache relief by exhibiting antimicrobial activity, which helps prevent infection and reduces the severity of toothache.
Limonene: Assists in toothache relief by its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in citral, a compound known for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe toothache pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Cymbopogon citratus contain oils that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in relieving toothache pain and preventing infections.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also a source of essential oils that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves when they are in their prime stage of growth for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested cymbopogon citratus leaves in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to remove moisture.
Grind the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the ground cymbopogon citratus powder with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with toothache because of its potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and cineole, work synergistically to numb the pain and reduce swelling in the affected area. As a natural antibacterial agent, it also combats underlying infections that may be contributing to the toothache. When applied topically or taken sublingually, Tasmanian blue gum tincture provides quick relief from throbbing pains, making it a popular remedy for individuals seeking an alternative to conventional painkillers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as eucalyptol and camphene, reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with toothaches.
Anesthetic: The analgesic properties of Eucalyptus globulus, specifically eucalyptol, help numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from toothache pain.
Antiseptic: The antimicrobial compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture, such as 1,8-cineole, prevent bacterial growth and promote healing of infected gums or abscesses that may be causing toothaches.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, a terpene, acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the area around the tooth to reduce pain and discomfort associated with toothache.
Catechins: Catechins, a type of phenolic compound, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, contributing to toothache relief.
Cineole: Cineole, another terpene, exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate pain and inflammation in the mouth and gums, providing relief from toothache.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their high eucalyptol content, which makes them effective in relieving pain and inflammation associated with toothaches.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, useful in treating toothache pain.
Roots: Contain sesquiterpene and flavonoid compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate toothache discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine 1 part eucalyptus globulus leaves with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture base.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar gently every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The eugenol present in cloves has been shown to numb the pain by reducing inflammation and soothing the nerve endings, providing quick relief from a toothache. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of clove oil help to combat any underlying infections that may be causing the discomfort.

Analgesic Properties: The eugenol present in cloves has a numbing effect on the mouth and gums, providing temporary relief from toothache pain.
Antioxidant Properties: Cloves contain antioxidants that help protect against free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum disease and other oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene acts as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and swelling associated with toothaches.
Eugenol: As a phenolic compound, eugenol has local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory properties, numbing the tooth and reducing discomfort.
Cinnamaldehyde: This aldehyde has antibacterial properties, helping to combat infection and promote healing in the affected tooth or gum area, reducing the severity of the toothache.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used due to their high content of eugenol, a natural analgesic and antiseptic compound that helps to numb the pain and reduce inflammation in toothaches.

Leaves: The leaves of Syzygium aromaticum contain eugenol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating toothache and promoting oral health.

Fruits: The fruits of Syzygium aromaticum contain essential oils that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the gums and teeth associated with toothache.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of syzygium aromaticum buds and 750ml of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the syzygium aromaticum buds and vodka in the jar and seal it tightly to prevent air from entering.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for 4 to 6 weeks to enable extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to separate the solids from the liquid.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in the gums and teeth. Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections that may be causing toothaches, reducing the risk of further infection and promoting healing.

remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds help with toothache by reducing inflammation and pain through their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Shogaols: These phenolic compounds are responsible for the analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate toothache pain.
Volatile oils (including zingiberene and bisabolol): These terpenes have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce toothache pain and prevent infection.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of active compounds, particularly gingerol and shogaol, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also utilized as they contain similar active compounds to rhyzomes, providing relief from toothache pain and inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and 750 milliliters of 25 ethanol solution for tincture base.
Chop the zingiber officinale rhizomes into small pieces and mix with the tincture base in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container.
Store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place and label it with the date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tinctures helps with toothache because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can ease pain and swelling. The antibacterial properties in herbal tea tinctures, such as peppermint, chamomile, and calendula, can also combat infection and reduce gum inflammation. Additionally, some tea tinctures have analgesic properties that numb the pain, providing quick relief from toothache. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Many herbal tea tinctures, such as those containing peppermint or chamomile, have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and gums.

Antibacterial properties: Herbal tea tinctures like those containing goldenseal or echinacea contain compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria, which can help prevent infections and alleviate toothache symptoms.

Anesthetic and analgesic properties: Certain herbal tea tinctures, such as those containing clove or wintergreen, contain natural anesthetics and analgesics that can help numb pain and reduce discomfort in the mouth.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which may contribute to toothache discomfort.

Catechins (specifically, epigallocatechin gallate, EGCG): EGCG has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate toothache pain and reduce swelling in the affected area.

Theaflavins: These compounds, formed during the fermentation process of Camellia sinensis, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with toothaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in camellianin and camelliasaponin, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate toothache pain.
Leaves: Containing camellianin and other compounds, the leaves help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with toothaches.
Barks: The bark's astringent and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and soothe toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 pound of dried camellia sinensis leaves and store them in an airtight container away from direct sunlight.
Combine the leaves with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks or 2 to 3 months.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place indefinitely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The antispasmodic compounds found in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, work to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms associated with toothaches. When applied topically or taken orally as a tincture, lemon balm helps to numb the affected area, providing rapid relief from pain and discomfort. Anxiolytic (stress-reducing) property: Lemon balm's ability to calm nerves and promote relaxation may help alleviate anxiety and stress-related discomfort associated with toothaches, making it easier for patients to tolerate necessary dental treatments.

remedy_constituents: Rosmarin酸 (Rosmarinic acid): This phenolic compound helps with toothache by reducing inflammation and pain through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Linalool: This terpene helps with toothache by acting as a local anesthetic, numbing the affected area and providing temporary pain relief.

Roots: Contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to ease toothache pain and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa flowers in the morning when they are dewy for optimal potency and fragrance.
Dry the melissa flowers using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Combine 1 part dried melissa flowers with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ajwain tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic properties, which effectively numb the pain and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in ajwain, such as thymol and carvacrol, have antibacterial properties that combat the underlying infection causing the toothache. Additionally, ajwain's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease pressure on the affected area, providing instant relief from toothaches caused by cavities, gum disease, or abscesses. remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Ajwain's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and eliminate bacteria responsible for tooth infections, thus alleviating pain and discomfort.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol has antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacterial infections that cause toothache, thereby reducing the severity of the pain.
Dill apiole: Dill apiole has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with toothache, providing relief from dental discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate toothache pain.
Leaves: They are used due to their analgesic and antiseptic properties, which help reduce pain and prevent infection.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh trachyspermum ammi leaves and flowers at the peak of their fragrance in the morning.
Dry the trachyspermum ammi leaves and flowers in a single layer at 50c for 2 hours.
Use a mortar and pestle to crush 50g of dried trachyspermum ammi into a fine powder.
Combine 250ml of 80% ethanol with 50g of trachyspermum ammi powder in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar every other day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb's active compounds, such as cuminaldehyde and limonene, work to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums and teeth. Additionally, cumin has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria and fungi that can cause toothaches. By applying a few drops of the tincture to the affected area or swallowing it, cumin helps to numb the pain, reduces inflammation, and promotes healing.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Cumin's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, reducing inflammation and pain associated with toothaches.
Anti-inflammatory property: Cumin's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain in the gums and teeth, providing relief from toothaches caused by gum disease or abscesses.

Anodyne property: The anodyne properties of cumin tincture help numb the affected area, temporarily relieving sharp toothache pains and making it easier to manage discomfort until medical attention can be sought.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cumin plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with toothaches.
Barks: The barks of the cumin plant contain tannins and flavonoids that help to numb the gums and relieve toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality cuminum cyminum seeds and vodka for preparation.
Measure one teaspoon of dried cuminum cyminum seeds for every four ounces of vodka.
Combine the measured cuminum cyminum seeds with vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for infusion.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids after 14 days of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander tinctures helps with toothache because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The coriander plant contains a compound called linalool, which has been shown to reduce pain and swelling when applied topically. When used as a tincture, coriander oil can be applied directly to the affected area, providing quick relief from toothache pain. Additionally, coriander's antibacterial properties help combat infection and promote healing in the gums and teeth, further reducing discomfort and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Coriander's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, providing relief from toothache.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of coriander tincture inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause tooth infections and inflammation, contributing to toothache relief.
Pain-relieving: Coriander's analgesic properties help alleviate pain associated with toothaches by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Coriander roots are used to make tinctures because they contain antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections causing toothaches.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriander leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their most potent and fragrant quantity.
Rinse the harvested coriander parts gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Combine 100 grams of dried coriander leaves and flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar tightly seal it.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing the mixture to steep and extract the active compounds slowly.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids then store the tincture in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The piperine in black pepper has been shown to reduce pain and swelling by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory compounds in the body. Antimicrobial property: Black pepper's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may be causing toothache, such as abscesses or gum disease, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory property: Black pepper's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce pain and swelling associated with toothache by reducing inflammation in the affected area, including the gums, teeth, and surrounding tissues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh black pepper berries from mature vines for their medicinal properties.
Wash the harvested berries gently in cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned berries using shade drying methods or low temperature ovens to prevent oxidation.
Combine 200g of dried black pepper berries with 400ml of high-proof vodka and store the mixture in a glass jar for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids after 14 days of infusion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Summer savory tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Analgesic: Summer savory's analgesic property helps alleviate pain by blocking or reducing the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing temporary relief from toothache discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of summer savory have a calming effect on toothaches, reducing inflammation and pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh satureja hortensis leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their highest potency for medicinal properties.
Dry the harvested satureja hortensis plant material using a food dehydrator or shade drying method within 24 hours of harvesting.
Combine one part dried satureja hortensis with three parts vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the plant material is completely covered by the liquid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14-21 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction of medicinal properties.

Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator at a dosage of 20-30 drops three times daily.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Creeping thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and redness in the gums and surrounding tissue, alleviating pressure on the affected tooth and reducing pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of creeping thyme contain essential oils and resins that help to reduce pain and inflammation in the gums and teeth.
Barks: The barks of creeping thyme contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against gum infections and tooth decay.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers in amounts of 100-200 grams for optimal potency.
Chop the collected plant material into small pieces and pack it into a clean glass jar tightly but not excessively.
Add high-proof vodka or grain alcohol to the jar at a ratio of 1 part plant material to 5 parts solvent with approximately 500ml capacity.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily and monitoring for fermentation.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store it in a dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, peppermint's menthol compound numbs the pain, providing instant relief from sharp pains and throbbing sensations associated with toothaches. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infection or inflammation causing the toothache, reducing swelling and promoting faster healing. As a result, herbal peppermint tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for alleviating toothache discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Peppermint's antibacterial properties help combat infection-causing bacteria that may be contributing to the toothache, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Analgesic: The menthol in peppermint has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to numb the pain of a toothache and reduce sensitivity.
Antispasmodic: Peppermint's antispasmodic properties can help relax muscles and calm spasms in the jaw, face, and mouth, which may be contributing to toothache pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have been traditionally used to make herbal remedies for toothaches due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling in the gums.

Buds: The buds of the peppermint plant contain high concentrations of menthol and other essential oils that are effective in numbing the mouth and reducing sensitivity, making them a popular choice for toothache relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the morning when dew is still present on the foliage.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 24 hours.
Combine dried peppermint leaves with 80 proof vodka and 10 grams of dried peppermint per 100 milliliters of vodka.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the container daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottles and store in a cool dark place for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, St John's Wort's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that may be causing the infection, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in the mouth and gums. By combining these actions, St John's Wort tinctures offer a natural and effective way to alleviate toothache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: The antiseptic property of St. John's Wort helps to reduce the risk of infection in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to toothaches.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory property of St. John's Wort helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches by reducing inflammation in the affected area.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of St. John's Wort helps to numb the pain caused by toothaches, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like hyperforin, which can help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with toothaches.
Barks: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective against oral infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Chop the plant material into small pieces using scissors or a knife in a clean environment.
Combine the chopped herb with 500ml of vodka in a glass jar leaving about 2-3 inches at top.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to extract.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with toothache because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe swollen gums and reduce pain caused by irritated dental nerves. The tannins present in burdock also have a numbing effect, providing instant relief from sharp pains and sensitivity. Additionally, burdock's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the toothache. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock root has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and pain associated with a toothache, making it easier to manage discomfort.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of burdock can help combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be causing the toothache, promoting healing and reducing risk of further infection.
Soothing and Calming: Burdock tincture has a calming effect on the mucous membranes and tissues in the mouth, which can help reduce sensitivity, pain, and discomfort associated with a toothache.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are often used to make tinctures because they contain inulin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with toothaches.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems that connect roots to the rest of the plant, have been traditionally used for their antimicrobial properties, making them effective against tooth infections that may cause toothache.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctium lappa roots and wash them thoroughly with water.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 80 proof vodka or other high-proof spirits to cover the root material completely.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks allowing it to steep and mature.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with toothache because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums and teeth. The tannins present in dandelion roots have antibacterial effects, which help combat bacterial infections that may be causing the toothache. Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in dandelion tincture possess antimicrobial properties that combat bacterial infections causing tooth decay, gum disease, and other oral health issues contributing to toothaches.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in morning dew or after evening rain for optimal potency.
Dry the collected plant material using a food dehydrator set at 40 degrees celsius for six hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind one part dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle with a small amount of glass jar.
Combine one tablespoon of the powdered plant material with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, seal tightly, and store at room temperature for four weeks.
After four weeks, filter the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, label, and store it in a cool place to preserve its potency for up to two years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for toothache are liquid extracts made from plants that help alleviate toothache pain and inflammation. They work by reducing swelling, killing bacteria, and numbing the affected area. Some of the best herbal tinctures for toothache include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Cinchona officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Syzygium aromaticum, Sanguinaria canadensis, Mentha x piperita, and Commiphora molmol. If left untreated, toothache can lead to severe pain, difficulty eating, and sleep disturbances, affecting one's quality of life and daily activities.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to toothache that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions such as gum inflammation, mouth ulcers, and sore gums. Tinctures made from herbs like chamomile, calendula, and echinacea can help soothe and calm the affected areas, reducing pain and inflammation. Other ailments treated with herbal tinctures include headaches, earaches, and joint pain, which can be relieved by formulas containing herbs like willow bark, peppermint, and turmeric.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with toothache is a blend of clove, myrrh, and calendula. Clove oil is known for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, while myrrh helps to reduce swelling and fight infection. Calendula, with its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, soothes the gum tissue and promotes healing. Mix equal parts of these tinctures in water and use as a mouthwash or apply topically to the affected area to experience relief from toothache pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It contains compounds like alkylamides and polyphenols that reduce swelling and ease pain, thereby alleviating toothache symptoms. Additionally, Echinacea's ability to stimulate the immune system may help fight off infections that cause toothaches. Some studies suggest that Echinacea may also possess analgesic properties, making it a potential natural remedy for temporary pain relief from toothaches. Its natural antibacterial action may also aid in preventing further infections.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with toothache.
Icariin: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate toothache pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
Caffeic acid: It has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent or treat infections that may be causing the toothache.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities that can help alleviate toothache symptoms.
Flowers: They contain a combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in soothing toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for best quality.
Dry the collected plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 1 to 2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried echinacea purpurea with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with toothache because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth and gums. By applying a few drops of tincture to a cotton ball and placing it near the affected tooth, the pain-relieving compounds can be absorbed through the mucous membranes, providing fast and effective relief from toothache. This natural remedy can be a welcome alternative to conventional painkillers.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and teeth.
Shogaols: Like gingerols, shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate toothache pain and inflammation.
Zingerone: This constituent has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the gums and teeth, making it effective in treating toothache.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for toothache tinctures due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are another significant part used for toothache tinctures, as they contain similar compounds to rhyzomes, which help to reduce pain and inflammation.
Buds: Buds are also used to make toothache tinctures, as they are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh zingiber officinale roots and chop them into small pieces for a 1:2 ratio.
Combine the chopped zingiber officinale roots with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Seal the glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for a minimum of 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks of maceration.
Transfer the filtered liquid to a glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator to preserve the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with toothache because it contains compounds like cineole, which have natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically to the affected area, the tincture can numb the pain and reduce swelling. The menthol content in Eucalyptus globulus tincture also helps to create a cooling sensation, which can provide temporary relief from the discomfort of a toothache. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate toothache pain and provide quick relief.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It helps to relieve toothache by acting as an analgesic and anesthetic, numbing the area around the affected tooth.
Euglobals: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and ease pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Volatile terpenes: They help to relieve toothache by reducing swelling and killing bacteria that may be contributing to the infection or inflammation causing the pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a compound with analgesic and antibacterial properties that help alleviate toothache pain.
Barks: The barks are also used for their eucalyptol content and their ability to reduce inflammation and fight infections, making them effective in treating toothache.
Roots: The roots of Eucalyptus globulus contain compounds that help to numb the pain and reduce inflammation, making them a popular choice for toothache relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves with a clean sharp knife to avoid contamination.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on paper bags or screens for 1-2 weeks.
Measure out 1 part of dried leaves to 2 parts of 80% vodka by volume in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures helps with toothache because it contains quinine, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound. The tincture's ability to numb the affected area and reduce swelling makes it an effective remedy for temporary toothache relief. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis tinctures may help to combat infection and reduce pain caused by tooth abscesses or gum inflammation. As a natural pain reliever, it provides a soothing and calming effect, offering quick and effective relief from toothache discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps with toothache by reducing inflammation and pain caused by bacterial infections, such as dental abscesses.
Quinovic acid glycosides: They help by reducing inflammation and pain associated with tooth decay and gum disease.
Quinovic acid aglycones: They help by exhibiting antimicrobial properties that combat bacterial infections and fight the causes of toothache.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis contain quinine, which helps to numb the toothache and reduce pain.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in quinine and other alkaloids, making them a popular choice for toothache relief.
Roots: The roots of Cinchona officinalis also contain quinine and other medicinal compounds that help to alleviate toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinchona officinalis leaves and stems in the early morning when the dew is still present for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested cinchona material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to prevent degradation.
Crush the dried cinchona material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Mix the cinchona powder with a 70% ethanol solution in a 1:2 ratio by weight to create a tincture.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to allow for extraction.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, have been shown to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections that often cause toothaches. Additionally, Calendula's analgesic properties can help alleviate pain and discomfort, providing temporary relief from toothache symptoms. The natural and non-invasive properties of Calendula tincture make it a popular herbal remedy for soothing toothaches and promoting oral health.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain associated with toothaches.
Kaempferol: This flavonoid has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort caused by toothaches.
Calendulin: This triterpenoid saponin has antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause tooth infections and contribute to toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used because they are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and mouth.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infection and reduce inflammation in the tooth and gum area.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate toothache pain and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom in the morning to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the calendula flowers in a single layer on paper bags for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Combine 1 part dried calendula flowers with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar to create tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures helps with toothache because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, cineole, has been shown to reduce bacteria and inflammation in the gums, relieving pain and discomfort. Additionally, its analgesic properties help to numb the affected area, providing quick relief from toothache pain. Barks: The bark's antimicrobial properties are utilized in tinctures to fight bacterial infections and promote healing in toothache cases.
Buds: The essential oils from the buds of Melaleuca alternifolia are used in tinctures to provide pain relief and reduce swelling in the gums and surrounding areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia on a dry day in the morning.
Chop 1 part of the harvested leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine 2 parts of 95% ethanol with 1 part of the chopped leaves in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum tinctures helps with toothache because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The tannins and phenolic acids present in the tincture help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums and teeth. Limonene: This terpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and swelling in the tooth and surrounding tissues, thereby providing relief from toothache.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds of Syzygium aromaticum are used to make tinctures for toothache because they contain a high concentration of compounds with analgesic and antibacterial properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Syzygium aromaticum are used to make tinctures for toothache because they contain oils with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate toothache pain.

Barks: The barks of Syzygium aromaticum are used to make tinctures for toothache because they contain compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of syzygium aromaticum buds at the peak of freshness for optimal potency and flavor.
Clean the buds thoroughly with water and then dry them using a food dehydrator for 8 hours.
Combine the dried buds with 2 cups of 95% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking and then strain it through cheesecloth.
Filter the tincture using a coffee filter to remove any remaining sediment and transfer it to glass bottles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sanguinaria canadensis tinctures helps with toothache because it contains compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active ingredients, including sanguinarine and dihydrochelirubine, work to reduce pain and swelling in the gums and surrounding areas. As a result, Sanguinaria canadensis tincture is often used as a natural alternative to alleviate toothache pain.

Dihydrosanguinarine: It contributes to the analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate toothache pain and discomfort.
Chelerythrine: It also has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing pain and inflammation associated with toothaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of isoquinoline alkaloids, specifically berberine, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves also contain berberine, which is responsible for their medicinal properties, including pain relief and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of Sanguinaria canadensis contain berberine, making them a potential source for toothache relief tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sanguinaria canadensis roots and clean them thoroughly in cold running water for about 5 minutes.
Chop the roots into small pieces weighing about 1 pound to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped root pieces with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a 1:5 ratio.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tinctures helps with toothache because it contains compounds like menthol and menthone that have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, these compounds can numb the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, menthol's cooling sensation can help to constrict blood vessels, which may help to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the nerves, providing quick relief from toothache pain. This natural remedy can be a helpful alternative to over-the-counter pain relievers.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps by numbing the tooth and surrounding area, reducing pain and discomfort.
Menthone: Helps by having anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and redness associated with toothaches.
Limonene: Helps by having antiseptic and antibacterial properties, preventing infection and promoting healing of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate toothache pain.
Stems: Used for their menthol content, which helps to numb the area and reduce pain.
Roots: Used for their antibacterial properties to help combat infection and reduce inflammation associated with toothache.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold water and pat them dry with a clean towel.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Commiphora molmol tinctures helps with toothache because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's main component, incensole, is known to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause infections, thereby reducing swelling and pain in the affected tooth. Additionally, the tincture's ability to stimulate the nervous system can help ease pain and discomfort associated with toothaches, providing quick relief and promoting overall oral health. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate toothache symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Boswellic acids: They help reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Terpenoids: They exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate toothache pain and inflammation.
Phenolic compounds: They have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent bacterial infections that may contribute to toothache.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Commiphora molmol are used to make tinctures due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with toothache.
Buds: The buds of Commiphora molmol are used to make tinctures due to their antiseptic and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and prevent infection associated with toothache.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of commiphora molmol roots and stems in the early morning after the dew has dried completely.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at 40 degrees celsius for 7 to 10 days.
Grind 10 grams of the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or coffee grinder.
Steep 1 part of the powdered plant material in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a dark glass jar for 4 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids then bottle the liquid.
